steps.title: Confirm required forms
steps.description: Use our interview to determine your exact form set. Pierce County requires official Washington State FL All Family forms. Verify your packet matches your situation (with or without minor children).

steps.title: Create a Pierce County e-filing account
steps.description: Register with Pierce County Superior Court e-filing. Requirements mirror King County but use Pierce County portals and fee schedules. Verify current fees on the Pierce County website.
steps.fees: Filing fee varies — verify current fee schedule at Pierce County Superior Court

steps.title: File the Petition and Summons
steps.description: Upload your Petition (FL All Family 103), Summons (FL All Family 119), and Confidential Information form (FL All Family 101). If you have children, include the Declaration Re Minor Children, Parenting Plan, and Child Support Worksheets.
steps.fees: Verify current filing fee at Pierce County Superior Court
steps.timeline: Same day once forms are ready
requiredDocuments: FL All Family 103 — Petition for Dissolution

steps.title: Serve your spouse
steps.description: Your spouse must be formally served with the Summons and Petition. File proof of service with the court.
requiredDocuments: Proof of Personal Service or Acceptance of Service

steps.title: Complete mandatory parenting seminar
steps.description: Both parents must complete a court-approved parenting seminar within 60 days of service. Keep your certificate of completion.

steps.title: Observe the 90-day waiting period
steps.description: Washington requires a minimum 90-day waiting period before final orders can be entered.

steps.title: Submit final orders
steps.description: File the Decree of Dissolution and any associated orders. Include Parenting Plan and Child Support Order if you have children.
requiredDocuments: FL All Family 185 — Decree of Dissolution
FL All Family 131 — Parenting Plan (if children)
